title: Inhibitor Switch, Accelerator Switch, Accelerator Position Switch, Stop Lamp Switch and Engine Speed Signal Circuit Checks

figures_detected.description: Circuit diagram showing IGN-SW with 10A fuse, Accel/Accel pos. sw., Inhibit SW, Stop lamp sw., N-CVT C/U and ECCS C/U connections with terminal numbers 10, 5, 2, 3, 15, 6, 11, 22, 2

Illustration of brake pedal depressed and released positions, with monitor display showing: CAR SPEED SEN 0 km/h, ENGINE REV 0 rpm, CLUTCH CRRNT* 0.0A, CLUTCH CRRNT 0.0A, PULLEY RATIO 0.00, ACCEL SW. OFF, THRTL OPEN SW OFF, AIR/C SIGNAL OFF, STOP LAMP SW ON. RECORD label shown.
Illustration of brake pedal positions and voltmeter connected to N-CVT C/U connector terminal 11 and body ground

content_blocks.text: Inhibitor Switch, Accelerator Switch, Accelerator Position Switch, Stop Lamp Switch and Engine Speed Signal Circuit Checks
Note: Refer to the inspection for each circuit for the inhibitor switch, accelerator switch and accelerator position switch circuit check procedures.

content_blocks.title: Stop Lamp Switch Circuit Check (Step A)

nodes.text:
Check stop lamp switch
1. Turn ignition switch "ON"
2. Select "E.C.U. Signals"
   • Check that brake switch goes "ON" and "OFF" when brake pedal is depressed and released. Check that the stop lamp switch also goes "ON" and "OFF" according to brake pedal operation
   — OR —
   • Check the voltage between N-CVT control unit terminal #11 and body ground when brake switch turns "ON" and "OFF"
   Brake pedal position | Voltage
   Depressed | 12 V
   Released | 0V

N.G. Check following items:
• Brake switch
  Refer to "Electrical Component Inspection"
• Check continuity of harness between N-CVT control unit and brake switch

model_notes: Applicable to N-CVT (automatic CVT) equipped models. Monitor display shows MONITOR / NO FAIL status. CLUTCH CRRNT* asterisk variant noted.
